title: Training the Judge
concept: The reward model acts as the judge during RLHF, learning to score model responses based on human preferences.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of training a reward model in RLHF?

options.text: To act as a judge that scores the quality of AI responses
To translate text into different languages

exercises.explain: The judge model learns from human feedback to assign scores (rewards) to responses, guiding the main AI model.

cards.title: Training the Judge
cards.body: How does an AI learn what humans actually prefer? It needs a judge! Before we can teach the main AI to behave, we first have to train a dedicated 'judge model' to recognize what makes a response good or bad.

cards.title: The Reward Model
cards.body: A reward model is a separate AI system trained specifically to evaluate and score the quality of responses generated by the main AI. It acts as a digital judge, grading outputs based on how much humans would like them.

cards.title: The Dog Show Judge
cards.body: Imagine training a dog show judge. Instead of teaching the dog how to perform, you are training the person holding the clipboard. By showing them pairs of performances, they learn exactly what a 'good boy' looks like so they can score future dogs correctly.

cards.title: Grading the Drafts
cards.body: If the main AI is asked to write a bedtime story, it might generate two drafts. The Reward Model looks at both, evaluates them based on its training, and awards Draft A a score of +1.8 and Draft B a score of -0.5, pointing out which path to follow.

cards.title: No Humans in the Room?
cards.body: People often think the main chatbot is graded directly by a live human every single time it learns. In reality, humans only train the judge (the reward model). Once trained, this digital judge grades the chatbot millions of times automatically!

cards.title: Creator vs. Judge
cards.body: Let's look at how the role of the Generator compares to the role of the Judge (Reward Model) during training.

right.text: Scores those drafts based on human preference.

left.text: Creates drafts, stories, and answers to prompts.

exercises.prompt: How does the reward model learn to become a good judge?

options.text: By comparing two AI responses and learning which one humans preferred
By writing its own essays from scratch
By search engine lookups for factual accuracy only
By copying random sentences from the internet

exercises.explain: The reward model is trained by looking at comparison data, learning to mimic which responses humans preferred over others.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The reward model's main job is to chat directly with human users.
exercises.explain: The reward model does not generate new conversational text; it only scores responses generated by other models.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of the judging system.
exercises.sentence: During training, the [blank] model acts as the judge to score outputs.
exercises.explain: The reward model acts as the judge, giving points or scores to guide the generator AI toward better behavior.

exercises.prompt: Which of these roles acts as the referee or judge during RLHF?

options.text: The Reward Model evaluating the answers
The human trainer writing the prompts

exercises.explain: The AI assistant is generating the primary content, while the human acts as the initial teacher. The Reward Model is the dedicated judge scoring the outputs.

exercises.prompt: What is the main role of the reward model during the RLHF process?

options.text: To write the final answers directly for the user
To score AI responses based on human preferences
To randomly generate new prompts for the training data
To run the web search engine for the chatbot

exercises.explain: The reward model's primary job is to score how well an AI response matches human preferences, which is then used to guide the generator model's behavior.
